one_time_description: Cold Lake Air Weapons Range Settlement - Government of Canada settlement of $60,500,000 for full and final settlement of the Cold Lake Air Weapons Range Claim, with $60,394,119 contributed to trust and $105,881 in legal fees
economic_notes: Buffalo River Mini Mart & Gas Bar Inc. is a wholly owned business entity accounted for by modified equity method with total revenue of $4,656,851. Nation is a member of Meadow Lake Tribal Council (9 Nations). COVID-19 impacted operations through social distancing, reduced gaming revenues, and office closures. ISC increased financial resources to facilitate service delivery during COVID-19.
notes: Fiscal year 2021 (ended March 31, 2021). Major one-time item: $60,500,000 Cold Lake Air Weapons Range Settlement received during year with $60,394,119 contributed to a trust not consolidated in financial statements. Nation was not in compliance with long-term debt financial covenants at year end. Audit was completed December 7, 2021, after the July 29, 2021 FNFTA submission deadline - Nation in breach of reporting requirement. Buffalo River Mini Mart & Gas Bar Inc. accounted for by modified equity method (not consolidated). Cold Lake Air Weapons Range Trust excluded from consolidation as controlled by independent Trustees. Capital segment expenses of $3,024,338 include housing and capital construction. expenses_housing set to 0 as housing is included within Capital segment ($3,024,338). total_government_transfers calculated as ISC + MLTC Program Services + MLHSDA + CMHC + MLTC CFS + Government of Saskatchewan + Northern Sports Culture Recreation District = 11,988,237 + 3,164,665 + 2,753,453 + 417,892 + 283,266 + 61,404 + 53,771 + 70,381 = 18,792,069 approximately; osr calculated as total revenue minus government transfers.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
